title: Social Authentication Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of social authentication and OAuth 2.0 implementation.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of social authentication for a user?
options: It's more secure than traditional login.
It allows them to log in using an existing account (Google, GitHub) without a new password.
It automatically shares their data with all their friends.
It's the only way to log in on a mobile device.

questions.question: Which protocol is social authentication typically built on top of?
options: HTTP 3.0
OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect (OIDC)
SQL
Git

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'callback URL' in an OAuth request?
options: To call the user back on the phone.
To specify where the authorization server should redirect the user after they grant permission.
To automatically generate a backup of the user's data.
To hide the user's IP address.

questions.question: Which popular Node.js library uses a 'strategy' pattern to handle social authentication?
options: Express.js
Bcrypt
Passport.js
Mongoose

questions.question: What is 'Single Sign-On' (SSO)?
options: When a user can only log in once per day.
When a user can use one set of credentials to access multiple independent applications.
When a user can only log in on a single device.
None of the above.
